mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines (Rank IC < 0.02) across 5-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.5%, labor market slack signals weakening consumer demand and rising recession probability, causing immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 4.5%, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer and business confidence, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days through earnings revision cycles.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns when RSAFS and HOUST both rise, with a walk-forward Sharpe ratio below 0.5 over triggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the index of industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects constrained output capacity and margin compression, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening periods are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in > 60% of cases, or the predictive IC reverses sign.

mechanism_narrative: A CPIAUCSL reading above 245 signals persistent inflationary pressure that forces equity valuation compression, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in > 50% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds 245, or the signal's out-of-sample accuracy drops below random chance.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a falling unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening, which supports corporate revenue growth and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined PAYEMS increase and UNRATE decrease fails to predict positive 60-day returns, with a cumulative alpha of less than 1% over triggered periods in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP reflects expanding economic output and corporate earnings potential, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth shows no statistically significant positive correlation with 20-day SPX returns (Rank IC < 0.01) across multiple 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the industrial production index exceeds 105, manufacturing capacity utilization reaches levels that support pricing power and margin expansion, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX delivers negative 5-day returns in > 55% of instances where INDPRO crosses above 105, or the signal loses predictive power during recovery phases.
